title: Oeceoclades humbertii
intro: Oeceoclades humbertii, a species of orchid, is a unique and rare plant native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and other health issues. The bioactive constituents of Oeceoclades humbertii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.

Overall, its classification is within the kingdom Plantae.
